title: Pseudo-reassign prose tool for updating method references after refactor
purpose: Updates method references in prose files after rename or move operations, preserving ID stability and ensuring accurate documentation.
module_context: Exports two main functions for updating method references in prose files: one for single updates and one for bulk operations with confirmation.

steps.content: Escape the file path for safe filesystem access.
Construct the full file path for the prose file.
Read the existing prose file content.
IF the file does not exist, return not_found.
Search for the method matching the old name, class, and parameters.
IF a matching method is found, update its name, class, and parameters.
IF no matching method is found, return not_found.
Write the updated content back to the prose file.
Return updated status.
Initialize the result object with counters and error array.
Apply the single reassignment operation.
Update the result counters based on the operation outcome.
IF an error occurs during the operation, record it in the errors array.
Trigger an incremental scan for the affected file in the database.
Return the final result object.

methods.name: pseudo_reassign_prose_bulk
methods.normalized_params: (project: string, mappings: ReassignMapping[], confirm: boolean): Promise<ReassignResult>

steps.content: Validate that bulk operation confirmation is enabled.
Initialize the result object with counters and error array.
Process each mapping in the list sequentially.
Apply the reassignment operation for each mapping.
Update the result counters based on each operation outcome.
IF an error occurs during any operation, record it in the errors array.
Trigger an incremental scan for all affected files in the database.
Return the final result object.
